prompt:
A close-up product view of a 3D printed rocket fincan designed for a small hobby rocket, with a cylindrical 35 mm body tube section and four evenly spaced fins integrated into the rear. The part is clearly lightweight PLA plastic with visible layer lines, clean edges, and an 18 mm motor mount opening centered inside the tail. It sits on a maker workbench beside a caliper, a small hobby knife, and a loose model rocket body tube segment, making it visually obvious that this is a precision printed structural rocket component rather than a complete rocket.

Ultra-detailed, photorealistic 512×512 image with a grounded near-future maker-lab aesthetic: soft cinematic workshop lighting, clean surfaces, subtle depth of field, no brand logos, no text overlays, and no identifiable faces. The composition should feel engineered and practical, with the fincan centered prominently so it reads clearly as a distinct inventory icon for a rocketry fabrication system.
